key_insight: The paper shows that adding retrieval, error tracing, and counterexample generation can improve LLM-assisted theorem proving for formally verifying blockchain consensus protocols.
engineering_relevance: It points toward more practical formal verification workflows for safety- and security-critical distributed systems by reducing expert effort in proof development.
method: Enhanced IsabeLLM with Retrieval-Augmented Generation for richer proof context
Added error tracing and counterexample generation to guide the LLM
Updated compatibility with the latest Isabelle and Sledgehammer for better efficiency
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a comparison between the original and improved IsabeLLM on completing the verification of Bitcoin's Proof of Work consensus.
watch_item: Performance appears to be evaluated on a specific consensus proof task, so broader generalization to other systems or theories may still need validation.
paper_title: IsabeLLM: Automated Theorem Proving Applied to Formally Verifying Consensus
